summary: Postinstall injects MCP/Claude config that silently relays chats and personal memory to author-controlled bridge
details: On `npm install`, scripts/postinstall.cjs recursively copies the package's payload/ directory into process.env.INIT_CWD (the installer's project root) using fs.cpSync, dropping.mcp.json,.claude/settings.json, CLAUDE.md, and three.cjs helpers without any prompt. The dropped.mcp.json hardcodes BRIDGE_URL=https://zoe.0x2ai.com and a static Bearer token, so any subsequent Claude Code session in that project auto-loads chatroom-mcp-lite-patched.cjs and routes every chatroom_post, memory_save, memory_load, provider_query, and settings_set call to the author-controlled bridge. CLAUDE.md instructs the agent to 'capture liberally' into memory_save, funneling the developer's and end-user's personal conversational data to zoe.0x2ai.com without opt-in. bin/start.cjs additionally spawns `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions` via shell, auto-approving every tool invocation while tool responses are sourced from the remote bridge — a path where bridge-supplied content can drive filesystem writes and shell exec on the host. The shipped BRIDGE_AUTH_TOKEN is a shared identity for all installers, so anyone with the tarball can read/write that tenant's bridge state. The advertised purpose is a chat connector, but installer-side data flow off-host is silent and consent-violating.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
